note: Period context to anchor each model year. Gas and new-car figures are approximate U.S. averages for the year, rounded, for a sense of scale.

1931.note: The Empire State Building opens as the nation weathers the Great Depression.

1936.note: Streamlining is the height of style, from locomotives to trailers.

1948.note: Postwar America takes to the new highways in record numbers.

1953.note: Route 66 is in its heyday and the family road trip becomes a ritual.

1954.note: Color TV goes on sale and the interstate era is dawning.

1955.note: Disneyland opens; tailfins and chrome define the American car.

1956.note: President Eisenhower signs the Interstate Highway Act.

1957.note: Sputnik launches the Space Age and the nation looks skyward.

1958.note: Explorer 1 becomes the first U.S. satellite in orbit.

1959.note: The jet age takes off and tailfins reach their peak.

1960.note: A new decade of optimism; the interstate network spreads fast.

1961.note: Alan Shepard becomes the first American in space.

1962.note: John Glenn orbits the Earth; Seattle hosts the World's Fair.

1964.note: The Ford Mustang debuts and Beatlemania sweeps America.

1965.note: The interstate system nears completion and the open road beckons.

1966.note: The muscle-car era roars and the highways are full.

1967.note: The Summer of Love; a restless nation takes to the road.

1968.note: A turbulent year — and families still escape down the highway.

1969.note: Apollo 11 lands on the Moon — and the returning crew is quarantined in an Airstream-built trailer.

1970.note: The first Earth Day; environmental awareness meets the open road.

1972.note: The RV boom is in full swing across America.

1974.note: The OPEC oil embargo spikes gas prices and brings 55-mph limits.

1976.note: The Bicentennial sends Americans touring their own country.

1979.note: A second oil crisis grips the nation.

1985.note: 'Back to the Future' hits theaters; the RV life is pure Americana.

1990.note: A new decade; SUVs and RVs surge in popularity.

2004.note: The Mercedes Sprinter arrives and modern van-life begins its rise.

2016.note: The tiny-house and overlanding movements go mainstream.

2020.note: A pandemic sparks a historic boom in RV and outdoor travel.

2024.note: Electric tow vehicles and off-grid solar reshape what a trailer can do.
